options: True — a short-lived service token obtained via POST to the auth/token endpoint is passed as X-Auth-Token on all subsequent API calls
False — DNA Center uses Basic Authentication on every request; no token exchange is required

questions.q: A DNA Center OBSERVER-ROLE token that successfully calls the global-credential endpoint indicates which security condition?
options: Normal behavior — OBSERVER tokens are designed to read all device credentials for monitoring purposes
An insufficient authorization check vulnerability — global credentials should require SUPER-ADMIN role and the OBSERVER token should receive a 403 Forbidden response

questions.q:
Long-lived DNA Center integration tokens should be rotated regularly and invalidated immediately after any credential exposure event.
True or False?
options: True — long-lived tokens (valid up to 6 months) represent persistent access risk; immediate invalidation limits the exposure window after a compromise
False — long-lived tokens are designed for stability; rotating them frequently causes automation outages and is not a security best practice

questions.q:
A WINWORD.EXE process spawning powershell.exe with an -EncodedCommand argument is a high-confidence indicator of malicious macro execution.
True or False?
options: True — Office applications spawning PowerShell with encoded commands is a known-bad pattern mapped to ATT&CK T1059.001 and is almost exclusively associated with malicious macros
False — encoded PowerShell commands from Office applications are commonly used by legitimate IT automation tools and have a high false positive rate

questions.q: In a phishing-to-domain-compromise kill chain, what MITRE ATT&CK technique does NTLM authentication from a workstation to a domain controller represent?
options: T1566.001 — Spearphishing Attachment (initial access via email)
T1550.002 — Pass the Hash (lateral movement using stolen NTLM credential material without knowing the plaintext password)

questions.q:
Cisco Umbrella detecting a DNS query to a newly-registered domain 45 seconds after PowerShell execution is a significant kill chain correlation signal.
True or False?
options: True — the temporal relationship between PowerShell execution and a newly-registered domain DNS query indicates C2 callback (ATT&CK T1071.001)
False — newly-registered domains are queried frequently in normal corporate browsing; the timing correlation is coincidental without additional evidence

questions.q: Which combination of data sources provides the highest-confidence detection of the phishing-to-domain-compromise kill chain?
options: Email gateway alone — detecting the malicious attachment before delivery prevents all downstream kill chain stages
Correlated telemetry from email gateway (attachment verdict), Secure Endpoint (process chain), and Umbrella (C2 DNS) — no single source sees the complete picture

questions.q:
XDR automated response playbooks can quarantine a compromised host within seconds of analyst approval, compared to manual containment that may take 30-60 minutes.
True or False?
options: True — XDR sends quarantine commands to Cisco Secure Endpoint via API, removing the host from the network in seconds once the playbook is triggered
False — endpoint quarantine always requires a direct connection to the endpoint and takes several minutes regardless of the management platform used

questions.q:
Cisco Silicon One's MACsec implementation operates at line rate (25.6 Tbps) without any throughput penalty on packet forwarding.
True or False?
options: True — MACsec GCM-AES-256 is hardware-accelerated in the Silicon One ASIC and does not impact forwarding throughput
False — MACsec encryption adds computational overhead that reduces throughput by approximately 15-20% on Silicon One

questions.q: An unauthorized gRPC telemetry subscriber on a Silicon One router can receive what type of sensitive operational data?
options: Only aggregate interface byte counts with a 5-minute polling interval — no real-time or per-flow data is exposed
Per-second interface utilization, per-flow statistics by destination AS, and forwarding table statistics — revealing the complete network traffic matrix

questions.q:
Mutual TLS (mTLS) on gRPC telemetry requires both a server certificate and a valid client certificate before a subscription is accepted.
True or False?
options: True — mTLS enforces bidirectional certificate authentication, ensuring only authorized collectors can subscribe to Silicon One telemetry streams
False — mTLS only validates the server certificate; client authentication on gRPC telemetry requires a separate API key mechanism

questions.q:
A compromised IOS XR control plane can reprogram Silicon One's P4 forwarding tables to redirect or drop traffic at wire speed.
True or False?
options: True — the control plane programs Silicon One's match-action tables via P4Runtime API; a compromised control plane has write access to all forwarding rules enforced at 25.6 Tbps
False — Silicon One's hardware includes a cryptographic lock that prevents the control plane from modifying forwarding tables without physical access

questions.q: MACsec on Silicon One provides protection against which specific threat on inter-datacenter links?
options: Denial-of-service attacks targeting the data plane — MACsec rate-limits incoming frames to prevent flooding
Eavesdropping and replay attacks on Ethernet frame payloads — MACsec encrypts the frame payload and authenticates the frame header at each hop

questions.q:
The HARVEST NOW, DECRYPT LATER (HNDL) threat requires adversaries to have a quantum computer before they begin collecting encrypted traffic.
True or False?
options: True — without a quantum computer, collection has no value; nation-states wait until decryption is feasible before archiving
False — HNDL posture involves collecting and archiving encrypted traffic today with the intention of decrypting it retrospectively once quantum computers become available

questions.q:
IKEv2 hybrid PQC (RFC 9370) combines classical ECDH-P256 with ML-KEM-768 so that the session key is secure against both classical and quantum attacks.
True or False?
options: True — the hybrid approach derives the session key from both shared secrets; breaking either algorithm alone is insufficient to recover the key
False — hybrid PQC uses ML-KEM-768 exclusively and deprecates ECDH-P256, which is removed from the IKEv2 proposal to prevent downgrade

questions.q:
Cisco IOS XR 7.9+ supports IKEv2 hybrid PQC with graceful fallback for peers that do not support ML-KEM.
True or False?
options: True — if the remote peer does not support ML-KEM-768, the IKEv2 exchange falls back to classical ECDH-P256, enabling phased deployment
False — enabling the PQC-HYBRID proposal breaks compatibility with all classical-only IKEv2 peers; both endpoints must upgrade simultaneously

questions.q: Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) and Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) both rely on computational hardness assumptions for their security guarantees.
options: False — QKD uses quantum physics laws for information-theoretic security (not computational hardness), while PQC relies on mathematical problems believed to be hard for quantum computers
True — both QKD and PQC depend on the difficulty of lattice-based mathematical problems standardized by NIST

questions.q:
MACsec GCM-AES-256 on Silicon One provides adequate near-term quantum resistance because Grover's algorithm only halves the effective key length, leaving 128 bits of quantum security.
True or False?
options: True — 256-bit symmetric keys reduced to 128-bit effective security by Grover's algorithm still provide an acceptable security margin for current threat timelines
False — Grover's algorithm breaks GCM-AES-256 completely; symmetric encryption provides no quantum resistance without post-quantum cipher suite upgrades

questions.q: True or False: HTTP encrypts data using TLS before sending it across the network.
options: True — HTTP uses TLS encryption
False — HTTP transmits data in plaintext

questions.q: The Firesheep exploit worked because Facebook used HTTP for browsing after logging in. What could an attacker steal to hijack another user's account?
options: The user's password from the login page
The user's unencrypted session cookie from ongoing HTTP traffic

questions.q: True or False: A login page that uses HTTPS guarantees your session stays secure even if the rest of the site uses HTTP.
options: False — a plaintext session cookie can be stolen to hijack your account after login
True — HTTPS on the login step protects the entire session

questions.q: Which part of the TLS handshake proves the server is who it claims to be?
options: The server sends its certificate, which the browser verifies against trusted Certificate Authorities
The server and browser agree on a symmetric AES-256 key using Diffie-Hellman

questions.q: What does a 'Not Secure' warning in your browser's address bar indicate?
options: The site's server is temporarily overloaded and requests may be slow
The page is using HTTP or has an invalid TLS certificate, meaning traffic is not encrypted

questions.q: True or False: Browser DevTools (F12) can only show HTML source code — they cannot reveal cookies or outgoing network requests.
options: True — DevTools is limited to viewing page source only
False — DevTools shows every request, response, cookie, and script the browser processes

questions.q: The British Airways Magecart attack injected 22 lines of JavaScript into the checkout page. Why couldn't customers visually detect the attack?
options: The injected script ran in the browser exactly like legitimate JavaScript — it was invisible to the user
The script disabled all browser security warnings before executing

questions.q: What is the browser's Same-Origin Policy (SOP) designed to prevent?
options: JavaScript on one page from reading data from a page on a different origin
Users from accessing HTTP pages when an HTTPS version is available

questions.q: True or False: HTML comments in page source are hidden from regular users and cannot be read without special tools.
options: True — HTML comments are stripped before the browser renders the page
False — anyone can view HTML comments using View Page Source or browser DevTools

questions.q: A Content Security Policy (CSP) header addresses which browser security problem?
options: It restricts which script sources a browser will execute, blocking unauthorized injected scripts
It forces all cookies to use the Secure flag so they are only sent over HTTPS

questions.q: In an IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) vulnerability, what does the server fail to do?
options: Verify that the requesting client is authorized to access the object ID it sent
Encrypt the response body before sending it back to the client

questions.q: True or False: In the client-server model, a client can become the server within the same HTTP request-response cycle.
options: False — in a given request-response exchange, the roles of client and server are fixed
True — modern HTTP/2 allows the client to push responses back to the server

questions.q: Which HTTP method is used to retrieve data from a server without modifying it?
options: POST — it sends a body with the request to retrieve filtered results
GET — it retrieves data and does not modify server state

questions.q: The Facebook Business Manager API IDOR in 2019 exposed users' phone numbers. What was the root cause?
options: The API returned any user's phone number given their user ID without checking if the caller was authorized to see it
An attacker cracked Facebook's encryption by exploiting a weak cipher in the TLS handshake

questions.q: True or False: A server can safely trust object IDs sent by a client as long as those IDs are numeric.
options: True — numeric IDs are harder to guess, so they are safe to use without authorization checks
False — the server must always verify whether the requester is authorized to access the object, regardless of ID format

questions.q: True or False: A TLS certificate with a valid signature from Let's Encrypt guarantees the website is legitimate and trustworthy.
options: True — Let's Encrypt only issues certificates to verified, trustworthy organizations
False — a Let's Encrypt certificate only proves domain control; phishing sites can obtain them too

questions.q: The DigiNotar hack allowed attackers to intercept Gmail for thousands of Iranian users. How was this possible?
options: Attackers used DigiNotar's compromised CA to issue fraudulent Google certificates accepted as valid by browsers
Attackers exploited a TLS protocol flaw that allowed them to decrypt AES-256 session keys

questions.q: When a browser encounters a certificate where the Common Name (CN) does not match the domain you are visiting, what does this indicate?
options: The server is using an older TLS version and needs to be upgraded to TLS 1.3
The certificate may have been reused from a different site, which is a potential man-in-the-middle indicator

questions.q: True or False: Clicking through a browser's 'Your connection is not private' warning is acceptable if you recognize the website.
options: True — the warning is often a false positive caused by expired certificates on legitimate sites
False — the warning means the server's identity is unverified; you may be communicating with an impersonator

questions.q: What was the purpose of Certificate Transparency logs, introduced after the DigiNotar incident?
options: To make all certificate issuance publicly auditable so unauthorized certificates can be detected
To allow browsers to cache certificates locally and reduce the number of CA verification requests

questions.q: True or False: The HttpOnly cookie attribute prevents JavaScript from reading the cookie via document.cookie.
options: True — HttpOnly blocks JavaScript access, so XSS attacks cannot steal the cookie
False — HttpOnly only prevents the cookie from being sent over HTTP; JavaScript can still read it

questions.q: Why does stealing a session cookie give an attacker full account access without knowing the user's password?
options: Session tokens are derived from the password, so possessing the token is equivalent to knowing the password
The server identifies the user solely by the session token; a valid token grants full access regardless of how it was obtained

questions.q: True or False: The SameSite=Strict cookie attribute is designed to protect against Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) attacks.
options: True — SameSite=Strict prevents the cookie from being sent in cross-origin requests
False — SameSite is only a performance hint and has no effect on cross-site requests

questions.q: The 2022 Slack breach involved stolen session cookies. Which security mechanism did this attack bypass entirely?
options: Two-factor authentication, because the session was already authenticated when the cookie was issued
TLS encryption, because the attacker used a man-in-the-middle attack on an HTTP connection

questions.q: What is session fixation, and how is it prevented?
options: Session fixation is when an attacker sets a known session token before login; prevented by regenerating the session token on login
Session fixation is when a cookie expires prematurely; prevented by extending the session lifetime on each request

questions.q: True or False: API authentication tokens should be placed in the URL query string for convenience since URLs are encrypted by HTTPS.
options: True — HTTPS encrypts the URL so placing tokens in query strings is safe
False — URL parameters appear in server logs, browser history, and referrer headers, exposing the token

questions.q: The Peloton API breach exposed private data for 4.5 million users despite the Peloton app displaying limited information. What was the root vulnerability?
options: The API returned the full user object with all private fields to any caller, even without authentication
Peloton stored passwords in plaintext, allowing attackers to log in as any user

questions.q: True or False: An API that requires authentication on its public endpoints is automatically safe from excessive data exposure vulnerabilities.
options: False — even authenticated APIs can return more data than the app displays, giving direct callers access to hidden fields
True — authentication ensures users only see the data they are authorized to access

questions.q: What does 'rate limiting' protect against in the context of API security?
options: It prevents attackers from enumerating all records by limiting how many requests a caller can make in a time window
It prevents attackers from intercepting API responses by throttling unencrypted traffic

questions.q: According to the OWASP API Security Top 10, what is the most common API vulnerability?
options: Broken Object Level Authorization — server returns data for any object ID without checking if the requester owns it
SQL Injection — attackers embed malicious queries in the JSON request body to extract database records

questions.q: True or False: Bandwidth and throughput are the same measurement — both describe the actual data rate achieved on a network connection.
options: False — bandwidth is the maximum capacity; throughput is the actual data rate achieved, which is always at or below bandwidth
True — bandwidth and throughput are interchangeable terms for measuring network speed

questions.q: If a server has a 10 Gbps internal link but only a 1 Gbps ISP uplink, what is the effective maximum bandwidth for internet traffic?
options: 10 Gbps, because the internal link is the primary path for all data
1 Gbps, because the ISP uplink is the slowest link in the chain and becomes the bottleneck

questions.q: True or False: Latency and bandwidth measure the same thing — a high-bandwidth connection always has low latency.
options: True — more bandwidth means data arrives faster, which reduces delay
False — bandwidth is pipe size; latency is delay time. Satellite internet can have high bandwidth but very high latency

questions.q: The 2023 Cloudflare DDoS peaked at 71 million requests per second from 30,000 IPs. What made Cloudflare's architecture resilient against this bandwidth attack?
options: Cloudflare filtered all traffic through a single hardened data center equipped with specialized DDoS hardware
Cloudflare's distributed architecture across 285+ data centers spread the load so no single location was overwhelmed

questions.q: A DDoS bandwidth saturation attack aims to achieve what outcome?
options: Flood the target's network connection with more traffic than it can handle, so legitimate traffic is dropped
Exploit a software vulnerability in the target's web server to crash the application process

questions.q: True or False: A ping result of 20ms means data takes 20ms to travel one way from sender to receiver.
options: False — ping measures round-trip time (RTT); 20ms RTT means approximately 10ms each direction
True — ping measures one-way latency from the sender to the remote host

questions.q: Which tool shows per-hop latency along the full network path to a destination, helping identify where congestion occurs?
options: traceroute — it shows the round-trip time to each intermediate router along the path
iperf3 — it measures maximum throughput between two hosts on the same network

questions.q: True or False: High latency on a connection always means there is a bandwidth problem that needs to be fixed by upgrading the link speed.
options: True — high latency is always caused by insufficient bandwidth and requires a faster link
False — latency can be caused by physical distance, processing delays, or congestion — not necessarily insufficient bandwidth

questions.q: The 2012 AWS US-East-1 outage showed how latency causes cascading failures. What is the core mechanism of a latency cascade?
options: One slow component causes upstream callers to queue waiting for it, backing up the entire call chain until the system halts
High latency causes packet loss, which triggers TCP retransmissions that permanently corrupt the session state

questions.q: Why does CDN (Content Delivery Network) usage reduce latency for end users?
options: CDNs compress all data before transmission, reducing the number of bytes that must travel across the network
CDNs serve content from nodes geographically close to the user, reducing the physical distance data must travel

questions.q: True or False: DNS cache poisoning corrupts a resolver's cache so that users are redirected to an attacker-controlled IP instead of the legitimate server.
options: True — the poisoned cache entry causes all users on that resolver to reach the attacker's server
False — cache poisoning only affects the speed of DNS lookups, not the IP addresses returned

questions.q: The 2008 Kaminsky attack exploited a weakness in DNS to make cache poisoning practical. What was the fundamental flaw?
options: The 16-bit DNS transaction ID provided too little entropy, allowing attackers to guess the right ID and inject a fake response before the real one arrived
DNS responses were transmitted without TLS encryption, allowing any router to read and modify them in transit

questions.q: True or False: DNSSEC prevents cache poisoning by encrypting DNS traffic so it cannot be intercepted in transit.
options: False — DNSSEC adds cryptographic signatures to DNS records so tampering is detectable, but it does not encrypt DNS traffic
True — DNSSEC uses AES-256 encryption to protect DNS responses from interception and modification

questions.q: If a DNS resolver's cache shows 185.220.101.50 for yourbank.com but the authoritative nameserver returns 198.51.100.77, what does this discrepancy indicate?
options: The authoritative server is misconfigured and has the wrong IP, so the cached version is correct
The resolver's cache is poisoned — the cached IP is an attacker-controlled address, not the legitimate server

questions.q: What is a practical advantage of DNS-over-HTTPS (DoH) compared to standard DNS for end users?
options: DoH prevents ISP interception and makes it harder for intermediaries to inject fake DNS responses
DoH eliminates the need for DNSSEC signatures because HTTPS already authenticates the DNS server

questions.q: True or False: The 'least connections' load balancing algorithm always routes each request to the next server in a fixed rotation, regardless of server load.
options: False — that describes round-robin; least connections routes to the server with the fewest active connections
True — least connections uses a rotation similar to round-robin but tracks connection counts

questions.q: A load balancer removes a backend server from the pool when the server fails its health check. What benefit does this provide?
options: It enables zero-downtime deployments by automatically removing unhealthy servers without manual intervention
It permanently blacklists the server IP so attackers cannot target it directly

questions.q: True or False: Placing an admin backend server in the same load balancer pool as public content servers is a security best practice because the load balancer controls who can reach it.
options: False — the load balancer routes traffic equally to all pool members, exposing admin backends to any traffic the LB receives
True — the load balancer acts as a security boundary that blocks unauthorized access to admin backends

questions.q: The 2022 Heroku breach demonstrated a risk specific to load balancers. What privileged capability made the load balancer a high-value target?
options: Load balancers have privileged network access to all backend servers in their pool, which attackers can exploit to reach internal services
Load balancers store TLS private keys in memory, allowing attackers to decrypt all traffic passing through them

questions.q: Which load balancing algorithm ensures the same client always reaches the same backend server on every request?
options: IP hash — the client's IP address is hashed to consistently map it to a specific backend server
Round-robin — it cycles through all servers in order, which naturally returns some clients to the same server over time

questions.q: The 2016 DNC hack began with a spear phishing email sent to which individual?
options: John Podesta, Hillary Clinton's campaign chairman
Hillary Clinton, the presidential candidate

questions.q: True or False: The display name in an email is a reliable way to verify who actually sent it.
options: True — email providers verify display names
False — the real sender is shown in the actual email domain, not the display name

questions.q: What is spear phishing?
options: A mass phishing campaign sent to millions of random recipients
A targeted phishing attack using personal details to appear credible to a specific victim

questions.q: Which email authentication failure is the strongest indicator that a message was not sent by the claimed domain?
options: DKIM, SPF, and DMARC all failing
The subject line containing urgent language

questions.q: True or False: Business Email Compromise (BEC) is the most financially damaging cybercrime category, costing $2.7 billion per year.
options: True
False — ransomware causes more financial damage than BEC

questions.q: True or False: Accounts protected by two-factor authentication are 99.9% less likely to be compromised than accounts without it.
options: True
False — the protection benefit is closer to 50%

questions.q: Why is SMS-based 2FA considered weaker than an authenticator app?
options: SMS codes expire faster than TOTP codes, making them harder to use
SMS codes can be intercepted via SIM swapping, while authenticator apps are immune to this attack

questions.q: How did the attacker compromise Uber's systems in the 2022 breach?
options: By exploiting a software vulnerability in Uber's VPN server
By spamming push notification approvals until a contractor clicked Approve out of frustration

questions.q: True or False: TOTP codes generated by authenticator apps work by communicating with the server in real time to receive each code.
options: True — codes are sent from the server to the app
False — both the server and app independently compute the same code using a shared secret and the current time

questions.q: What attack is number-matching 2FA specifically designed to defeat?
options: MFA fatigue attacks, where attackers spam push notifications hoping for an accidental approval
SIM swapping attacks that redirect SMS codes to the attacker's phone

questions.q: True or False: A long passphrase made of four random words is generally stronger than an 8-character password with complex substitutions like P@ssw0rd.
options: True — length increases entropy exponentially and substitutions are in attackers' dictionaries
False — complex character substitutions make shorter passwords more resistant to cracking

questions.q: What was the most significant outcome of the 2009 RockYou breach?
options: It proved that bcrypt hashing makes passwords uncrackable at scale
32 million passwords stored in plaintext were exposed, creating rockyou.txt — the most used password cracking wordlist

questions.q: At 100 billion guesses per second, approximately how long would it take to crack a password with 71 bits of entropy?
options: A few seconds to a few minutes
Approximately 750 years

questions.q: True or False: Salting a password hash prevents rainbow table attacks by ensuring two users with the same password have different stored hashes.
options: True
False — salting only slows down brute-force attacks, not rainbow table lookups

questions.q: Which hashing algorithm is most appropriate for storing user passwords in a modern application?
options: MD5 or SHA-1, because they are fast and widely supported
bcrypt or Argon2id, because they are deliberately slow and resistant to GPU-based cracking

questions.q: True or False: On an open WiFi network, an attacker must send packets to your device to capture your traffic.
options: True — active injection is required to intercept traffic
False — passive listening captures all unencrypted frames without sending a single packet

questions.q: What protection does HTTPS provide on a public WiFi network?
options: It encrypts the content of web traffic, though the destination domain remains visible via SNI
It fully encrypts all traffic including DNS queries and hides the destination domain entirely

questions.q: How did the Darkhotel APT group compromise executives on hotel WiFi networks?
options: By stealing hotel WiFi passwords using brute-force attacks against the router
By sitting on the hotel network as a man-in-the-middle and injecting malicious fake software update prompts into HTTP connections

questions.q: True or False: On WPA2 personal WiFi, if you know the network password you can decrypt other users' traffic.
options: True — all devices on a WPA2 personal network share the same encryption key
False — WPA2 uses per-session keys that prevent other users from decrypting your traffic

questions.q: Which option provides the strongest protection when you must use an untrusted network?
options: Using HTTPS-only websites, which encrypts content and hides all metadata
Using your phone's mobile hotspot, which creates an isolated 4G/5G channel away from shared WiFi

questions.q: True or False: Social engineering attacks require exploiting a software vulnerability to succeed.
options: True — attackers need a technical exploit entry point
False — social engineering manipulates people directly, bypassing all technical controls

questions.q: In the Facebook/Google BEC scam, how did attacker Evaldas Rimasauskas steal over $120 million?
options: By hacking into the companies' financial systems using malware
By sending fake invoices impersonating a real supplier that both companies regularly paid

questions.q: Which psychological lever does urgency exploit in social engineering attacks?
options: It overrides the victim's rational decision-making by creating panic and time pressure
It makes the victim feel they are being watched and must comply to avoid punishment

questions.q: True or False: Legitimate IT support staff will sometimes need your password to help diagnose and fix account issues.
options: True — IT teams require credentials to access accounts for troubleshooting
False — legitimate IT never needs your password; a request for it is a red flag

questions.q: What is the correct way to verify the identity of someone claiming to be from your IT department or a security agency?
options: Ask them for their employee ID number and write it down for your records
Hang up and call back using a known official number, not the one they provided

questions.q: True or False: Most real-world cyberattacks exploit zero-day vulnerabilities that have no available patch.
options: True — attackers primarily rely on unknown vulnerabilities
False — most attacks exploit known, patchable vulnerabilities because most systems are poorly patched

questions.q: The WannaCry ransomware infected 200,000 computers in 2017. What was the key factor that made this possible?
options: The attackers discovered a novel zero-day that no one knew about
A patch for the exploited vulnerability (MS17-010) had been available for two months but was not applied

questions.q: What does a CVSS score of 9.8 indicate about a vulnerability?
options: It is a critical severity vulnerability, likely allowing remote code execution with no authentication
It is a moderate severity vulnerability that requires local access to exploit

questions.q: True or False: Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228) required attackers to have prior access to a system before they could exploit it.
options: True — Log4Shell required authenticated access to trigger
False — sending a single specially crafted string that got logged was enough to trigger remote code execution

questions.q: What is a zero-day vulnerability?
options: A vulnerability unknown to the vendor for which no patch yet exists
A vulnerability that was patched on the same day it was publicly disclosed

questions.q: True or False: A data breach only puts you at risk if you made a security mistake with that company.
options: True — breaches only expose users who had weak passwords
False — your data is exposed whenever the company holding it is breached, regardless of your own security practices

questions.q: What is credential stuffing?
options: An attack where stolen username and password pairs from one breach are tested automatically against other sites
A technique for guessing passwords by trying every possible combination of characters

questions.q: How long did Yahoo's 2013 breach of 3 billion accounts go undetected before disclosure?
options: Approximately three years, discovered in 2016
Approximately six months, discovered in early 2014

questions.q: True or False: The Have I Been Pwned API uses k-anonymity, meaning your actual password is never transmitted when checking if it has been exposed.
options: True — only the first 5 characters of the SHA-1 hash are sent to the API
False — the full password hash is sent to the server for comparison

questions.q: Why are security question answers particularly dangerous when exposed in a data breach?
options: Security questions are harder to change than passwords, and the answers often remain valid across accounts indefinitely
Security question answers are stored in plaintext, making them easier to crack than hashed passwords

questions.q: Given the URL https://monterey-aquarium.org.phish-site.ru/tickets — what is the actual domain?
options: monterey-aquarium.org, since that appears first in the URL
phish-site.ru, because the real domain is always the rightmost portion before the first slash

questions.q: True or False: An HTTPS padlock in the browser means a website is safe and not a phishing site.
options: True — HTTPS proves the site is legitimate
False — HTTPS only proves the connection is encrypted; phishing sites routinely use HTTPS with valid certificates

questions.q: In Operation Aurora, how did attackers compromise Google employees' devices?
options: By sending email attachments that required the victim to manually download and run a file
By directing targets to a malicious website that exploited an Internet Explorer zero-day via a drive-by download requiring no additional clicks

questions.q: True or False: URL shorteners like bit.ly always reveal their final destination in the link preview before you click.
options: True — all major URL shorteners display the destination URL on hover
False — URL shorteners hide the real destination; you should expand them using tools like curl or unshorten.me before clicking

questions.q: Which method is most reliable for safely checking whether a suspicious URL is malicious?
options: Opening the URL in a private or incognito browser window to limit tracking
Scanning the URL with VirusTotal or Google Safe Browsing before visiting it

questions.q: True or False: Most apps genuinely need always-on location access to function correctly.
options: True — always-on access is required for apps to deliver timely location-based features
False — almost no apps need always-on location; 'While Using' is sufficient for the vast majority

questions.q: How did Cambridge Analytica harvest data from 87 million Facebook users without most of them consenting?
options: They hacked Facebook's database directly using a SQL injection vulnerability
A personality quiz app exploited Facebook's API to collect not just the quiz-taker's data but also all of their friends' data

questions.q: What is the principle of data minimization in the context of privacy?
options: Compressing personal data files so they take up less storage space on servers
Sharing only the minimum amount of personal information necessary, because every piece shared becomes a potential breach exposure

questions.q: True or False: Data brokers compile profiles including addresses, phone numbers, and relatives' names that can be used for targeted spear phishing.
options: True
False — data brokers only sell anonymized aggregate statistics, not individual profiles

questions.q: Why is sharing your real phone number for SMS 2FA a potential privacy and security risk?
options: Phone numbers can be used for SIM swapping attacks, where attackers transfer your number to steal SMS codes
Phone numbers allow apps to track your location without needing location permission

questions.q: According to the NIST SP 800-61 incident response framework, what should be your first action after discovering a compromise?
options: Containment — stop the attacker's active access before investigating the full scope
Root cause analysis — understand how the attacker got in before taking any other action

questions.q: True or False: Changing your password after an account compromise is sufficient to fully evict an attacker.
options: True — a password change immediately terminates all attacker access
False — attackers can maintain access through OAuth app tokens and email forwarding rules that survive password resets

questions.q: What was the entry point for the Colonial Pipeline ransomware attack?
options: A zero-day vulnerability in the pipeline's industrial control system software
A single compromised VPN password with no 2FA enabled on the account

questions.q: True or False: During incident response, documenting the timeline and evidence should wait until after full recovery is complete.
options: True — documentation is a post-recovery activity that should not distract from remediation
False — documentation should happen throughout the response; it supports root cause analysis, legal action, and improving defenses

questions.q: Which of these is an Indicator of Compromise (IOC) that should trigger immediate investigation?
options: Receiving a software update notification from a known vendor through the app's built-in update mechanism
Receiving a password reset email you did not initiate, or seeing unfamiliar devices listed in your account sessions

questions.q: True or False: In the 2021 Facebook outage, engineers could not fix the problem remotely because the routing tables had been deleted.
options: True — they had to physically go to the data center
False — they fixed it remotely within minutes

questions.q: What is the internet, according to the stage content?
options: A single global network owned by a consortium of governments
A global mesh of millions of smaller interconnected networks

questions.q: True or False: In a mesh topology, all devices connect through a single central hub.
options: True — the hub is the central coordinator
False — devices connect directly to each other, with no single point of failure

questions.q: Which command reveals the intermediate routers between your device and a destination?
options: traceroute (or tracert on Windows)
arp -a

questions.q: A routing table is described as the network's 'map.' What happens when that map is lost?
options: Data travels more slowly but still reaches its destination via backup paths
Routers cannot forward packets and the network becomes unreachable

questions.q: True or False: Your device's private IP address (e.g., 192.168.1.5) is directly visible to servers on the public internet.
options: True — every server you contact sees your private IP
False — your router's public IP is what the internet sees, via NAT

questions.q: Why did IANA exhaust all IPv4 blocks in February 2011?
options: IPv4's ~4.3 billion addresses were insufficient for the number of devices connecting to the internet
IPv6 was officially adopted and replaced IPv4 entirely

questions.q: Which of the following is a private IP address range that never appears on the public internet?
True or False: IPv6 was designed specifically to solve the IPv4 address exhaustion problem.
options: True — it uses 128-bit addresses providing 340 undecillion unique addresses
False — IPv6 was designed for speed, not address space

questions.q: A packet traveling across the internet carries which two essential addressing fields?
options: Source IP and destination IP
Source MAC address and destination MAC address

questions.q: True or False: When a large file is sent over the internet, it travels as a single continuous stream rather than being split up.
options: True — the internet sends files whole to preserve integrity
False — data is broken into ~1,500-byte packets that are reassembled at the destination

questions.q: What does TCP use to guarantee that lost packets are retransmitted?
options: ACK (acknowledgment) packets — the receiver confirms each packet received
Sequence numbers alone — the sender retransmits everything if any number is skipped

questions.q: Which protocol is faster but provides no guarantee of delivery or ordering?
options: TCP, because it uses a three-way handshake
UDP, because it skips acknowledgments and connection setup

questions.q: True or False: The 2016 Mirai DDoS attack succeeded by exploiting an unpatched software vulnerability in Dyn's servers.
options: True — Mirai used a zero-day exploit to crash the servers
False — Mirai flooded Dyn with packet volume from hijacked IoT devices, overwhelming connection tables

questions.q: Why does the internet's packet design mean a lost packet does not require resending an entire file?
options: Each packet has a sequence number, so only the missing packet needs to be retransmitted
The sender always sends every packet twice as a built-in redundancy measure

questions.q: True or False: A router knows the complete end-to-end path a packet will take before forwarding it.
options: True — routers coordinate in advance to pre-plan full routes
False — each router only decides the next hop based on its local routing table

questions.q: When a packet's destination IP matches multiple entries in a routing table, which entry does the router use?
options: The most specific match (longest prefix)
The first entry listed in the routing table

questions.q: What role does BGP play in internet routing?
options: It synchronizes routing tables between networks so routers know how to reach each other's IP ranges
It encrypts routing table entries to prevent unauthorized modification

questions.q: True or False: During the 2010 China Telecom BGP incident, encryption was broken and private data was decrypted.
options: True — China Telecom decrypted all traffic that passed through their network
False — traffic was rerouted and readable only if unencrypted, but no encryption was broken

questions.q: What security problem does BGP have that made the 2010 China Telecom incident possible?
options: BGP has no built-in authentication — any router can announce routes for any IP range
BGP uses weak encryption that can be cracked by nation-state attackers

questions.q: True or False: DNS translates human-readable domain names into IP addresses that computers use to connect.
options: True — this is DNS's core function
False — DNS translates IP addresses into MAC addresses for local delivery

questions.q: What is a TTL (Time To Live) in a DNS response?
options: How long the resolved IP address is cached before a fresh lookup is needed
How many router hops the DNS query can make before expiring

questions.q: In the 2016 Dyn attack, why were major websites like Twitter unreachable even though Twitter's own servers were running normally?
options: Twitter's servers were secretly taken down by a secondary attack that went unreported
Dyn's DNS servers were taken offline, so browsers couldn't resolve Twitter's domain to an IP address

questions.q: True or False: DNS cache poisoning works by replacing a legitimate IP address in your DNS cache with a malicious one.
options: True — the victim then connects to the attacker's server while typing the correct domain
False — DNS cache poisoning only slows down DNS lookups, it does not redirect traffic

questions.q: Which part of the DNS hierarchy responds first when your DNS resolver cannot find a cached answer?
options: A root name server, which refers the resolver toward the correct TLD server
The authoritative name server for the specific domain you are querying

questions.q: True or False: A device's MAC address is used to route traffic across the global internet.
options: True — MAC addresses are the primary identifier used by internet routers
False — MAC addresses operate at the local network layer; IP addresses handle internet routing

questions.q: What do the first three bytes of a MAC address identify?
options: The manufacturer of the network interface card (the OUI)
The device's assigned IP address range

questions.q: ARP has no authentication. What attack does this enable on a local network?
options: ARP spoofing — an attacker sends fake ARP replies to become a man-in-the-middle
ARP flooding — an attacker overwhelms the router with too many address requests

questions.q: True or False: Modern operating systems use MAC address randomization to prevent tracking across different WiFi networks.
options: True — a random MAC is used for each new network to prevent location tracking
False — MAC randomization is only used on mobile devices, not laptops or desktops

questions.q: Why does ARP spoofing pose a serious threat even on encrypted networks?
options: ARP spoofing redirects traffic to the attacker's machine; unencrypted traffic is fully readable, and even encrypted traffic reveals connection metadata
ARP spoofing can break TLS encryption by forging certificates alongside the fake ARP reply

questions.q: True or False: Firewall rules are evaluated top-to-bottom, and the first matching rule determines the packet's fate.
options: True — rule order is critical; a broad rule above a narrow rule shadows the narrow one
False — all rules are evaluated and the most specific match wins, regardless of order

questions.q: What is the 'default-deny' security principle in firewall design?
options: Block all traffic not explicitly permitted by a rule
Deny traffic only from known malicious IP addresses

questions.q: What is the key difference between a stateless and a stateful firewall?
options: A stateful firewall tracks connection state, allowing return traffic for established sessions without opening inbound ports
A stateless firewall is faster because it uses hardware acceleration; stateful firewalls use software only

questions.q: True or False: The 2003 SQL Slammer worm could have been stopped by blocking a single UDP port at the firewall.
options: True — blocking UDP port 1434 would have completely prevented Slammer from spreading
False — Slammer spread through multiple ports simultaneously, making firewall rules ineffective

questions.q: When a broad ALLOW rule appears above a specific DENY rule in a firewall ruleset, what happens to the DENY rule?
options: The DENY rule never fires — all matching traffic is already allowed by the rule above it
The DENY rule still applies to traffic that matches it, because specific rules always override broad ones

questions.q: True or False: Port 443 is the standard port for encrypted HTTPS web traffic.
options: True — HTTPS listens on port 443 by default
False — HTTPS uses port 80 and HTTP uses port 443

questions.q: How did WannaCry ransomware spread to 200,000 computers without requiring any user action?
options: It automatically scanned for open port 445 (SMB) and exploited an unpatched vulnerability to self-propagate
It was distributed as an email attachment that users had to open to trigger the infection

questions.q: Why is finding port 3306 (MySQL) open and reachable from the internet considered a critical security finding?
options: Database ports should only be accessible internally; public exposure allows direct attack against the database
Port 3306 is deprecated and its presence indicates outdated software that will crash under load

questions.q: True or False: Running a port scan with default nmap settings guarantees you have checked all 65,535 ports.
options: True — nmap always scans every available port by default
False — default nmap scans only the top 1,000 common ports; full range requires the -p- flag

questions.q: What does a port number in a network packet tell the receiving operating system?
options: Which application or service should handle the incoming data
Which network interface the packet arrived on

questions.q: True or False: Devices on the same subnet can communicate directly without passing through a router.
options: True — same-subnet traffic stays local and never crosses a router boundary
False — all traffic must pass through the router, even between devices on the same subnet

questions.q: How many usable host addresses does a /24 subnet provide?
options: 254 hosts
65,534 hosts

questions.q: In the 2013 Target breach, why did the attacker's access to an HVAC vendor account lead to credit card theft?
options: Target's network lacked sufficient segmentation, so the HVAC vendor's subnet had a direct route to the point-of-sale systems
The HVAC vendor's account had administrator privileges on Target's payment servers

questions.q: True or False: Proper network segmentation limits the blast radius of a breach by preventing an attacker from moving freely between subnets.
options: True — subnets with firewall rules between them contain lateral movement
False — a determined attacker can always move between subnets once inside the network perimeter

questions.q: If two devices have IPs of 10.0.2.15 and 10.0.3.8, both with a /24 mask, how do they communicate?
options: They must route through a router or firewall because they are on different /24 subnets
They communicate directly because they share the same 10.0.x.x address space

questions.q: True or False: TCP establishes a connection using a three-way handshake (SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK) before sending data.
options: True — this handshake confirms both sides are ready before any data is exchanged
False — TCP sends data immediately and negotiates connection parameters in the first data packet

questions.q: Why does the TCP SYN flood attack work as a denial-of-service technique?
options: Servers allocate memory for each incoming SYN and wait for an ACK that never arrives, exhausting their connection table
Servers are forced to decrypt each SYN packet, which consumes excessive CPU resources

questions.q: True or False: UDP is preferred for video calls and real-time gaming because it guarantees delivery of every packet.
options: True — UDP's delivery guarantee keeps video frames consistent
False — UDP is preferred because its low overhead makes it faster; a dropped frame is acceptable

questions.q: In the TCP/IP model, what is the role of the Transport layer?
options: Providing reliable (TCP) or fast (UDP) delivery between applications on different hosts
Translating domain names to IP addresses for applications

questions.q: According to the stage content, what is fundamentally true about every network vulnerability?
options: It exploits a flaw or unintended behavior in a protocol's design or assumptions
It requires physical access to network hardware to be effective

questions.q: MITRE ATLAS is the AI/ML analog of ATT&CK — it covers attacks on AI and machine learning systems rather than traditional IT infrastructure.
options: True
False

questions.q: Which type of ML API response provides the most information for mapping decision boundaries?
options: Confidence scores / probability distributions
Hard labels (top-1 prediction only)

questions.q: Passive ML reconnaissance includes examining model cards on HuggingFace and published research papers about the target model.
options: True
False

questions.q: In the Clearview AI case, what reconnaissance method was used to build the training dataset?
options: Querying private law enforcement databases
Scraping 3+ billion public images from social media

questions.q: A model that returns very high confidence (>0.95) on most inputs, including adversarial examples, is best described as:
options: A well-calibrated model with strong robustness
An overconfident model — likely a CNN-based architecture

questions.q: A substitute (proxy) model is trained by querying the target API and using the API's responses as training labels — no access to the target's weights is needed.
options: True
False

questions.q: What is the 'transfer rate' in a substitute model attack?
options: The speed at which API queries are sent to avoid rate limits
The percentage of adversarial examples crafted on the substitute that also fool the real target

questions.q: Returning only hard labels (no confidence scores) from an ML API is an effective defense against substitute model training.
options: True
False

questions.q: The ART (Adversarial Robustness Toolbox) is used for:
options: Encrypting model weights to prevent extraction
Crafting and evaluating adversarial attacks and defenses in ML systems

questions.q: In the GPT-4 model extraction research, what was inferred about GPT-4's architecture using approximately $200 in API queries?
options: Its hidden dimension size by analyzing the output embedding space
Its full weight matrix by decoding API token probabilities

questions.q: ML supply chain attacks can be executed simply by uploading a convincing lookalike model to a public registry like HuggingFace.
options: True
False

questions.q: Why is `torch.load()` dangerous when loading models from untrusted sources?
options: It permanently deletes model weights after loading
It uses Python's pickle format, which can execute arbitrary code on load

questions.q: Pinning a model by its SHA256 hash instead of just its name protects against supply chain typosquatting attacks.
options: True
False

questions.q: In the 2024 JFrog discovery, what technique did malicious HuggingFace models use to execute code?
options: SQL injection via model metadata fields
Pickle deserialization exploit triggered on torch.load()

questions.q: The recommended PyTorch parameter to prevent pickle-based code execution when loading a model file is:
options: `weights_only=True` in torch.load()
`safe_mode=True` in torch.load()

questions.q: Black-box access — where an attacker can only send inputs and observe outputs through an API — is sufficient to craft adversarial examples against a target model.
options: True
False

questions.q: The HopSkipJump attack is notable because it finds adversarial examples using only:
options: Full white-box access to the model's gradients and weights
The model's final hard label output — no confidence scores or gradients needed

questions.q: In the ChatGPT training data extraction attack, researchers prompted the model to 'repeat the word poem forever' to trigger a divergence that emitted memorized training data.
options: True
False

questions.q: Differential privacy during training is a defense against which black-box attack capability?
options: Membership inference and training data extraction from model outputs
Decision boundary mapping via confidence score analysis

questions.q: LLM black-box attacks differ from vision model attacks primarily because:
options: LLM inputs are discrete tokens, making gradient-based methods inapplicable
LLMs cannot be queried through APIs and require local model access
